claim: Boundary snapshot implementation in src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts correctly combines git ls-files (tracked/cached/other) with fallback walk and excludes allowed artifact roots.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z to get tracked, cached, and untracked files
evidence.why: This is the primary mechanism for detecting mutations in git-backed projects.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() provides recursive fallback for non-git projects, excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: Ensures boundary coverage for projects without version control.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() combines git and walk results, excludes allowed roots via isWithin() check, and stores SHA256 digests
evidence.why: Core boundary detection logic that covers all files except artifact directory.

evidence.summary: Combines git and walk results: [...new Set([...git, ...walkFiles(projectRoot)])]
evidence.why: Ensures both git-tracked and untracked files are included in the snapshot.

evidence.summary: Excludes allowed roots: if (allowed.some((root) => isWithin(absolute, root))) continue;
evidence.why: Ensures artifact writes are not flagged as mutations.

evidence.summary: Runtime captures boundary before/after agent execution and reports violations with specific file changes
evidence.why: Demonstrates the boundary audit is enforced during agent dispatch.

evidence.summary: Test: boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary violations are detected and blocked.

evidence.summary: Test: git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations
evidence.why: Confirms untracked file detection works in git repos.

evidence.summary: Test: git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs
evidence.why: Proves .env and other gitignored files are detected when modified.

evidence.summary: Test: non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts
evidence.why: Validates fallback walk works for non-git projects and artifact exclusion works.

evidence.summary: excludedDir() excludes .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache from walk
evidence.why: Shows the implementation respects standard ignore patterns.

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundary snapshot before agents run: buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir])
evidence.why: Evidence that allowed roots are passed to exclude artifact directory.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() detects added, modified, and deleted files by comparing SHA256 digests
evidence.why: Shows the mechanism for detecting specific mutation types.

risks: If git ls-files behavior changes in future git versions, boundary detection may miss untracked files
Filesystem walk in large non-git projects may have performance implications
Dirty working tree stress-test with real Claude agents has not been verified
missing: Verification that boundary audit works correctly when run in a real dirty working tree with actual Claude -p agents (current test suite uses mocked agents and temporary directories)
Evidence that the implementation correctly handles edge cases like git submodules, worktrees, or large repos
Confirmation that SHA256 digest computation handles all file types (binary, symlinks, etc.) correctly

result.notes: The implementation correctly covers git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walk. All tests pass. The remaining gap is real-world stress-testing with actual Claude agents in the current dirty working tree.
